name: Edge AI Deployment
note: Estimates based on Azure retail pricing. Actual costs depend on usage, region, reserved instances, and enterprise agreements.

services.name: Azure IoT Hub
services.purpose: Device management, model deployment orchestration, telemetry ingestion, edge-to-cloud sync

services.name: Container Instances
services.purpose: Cloud-side model quantization, ONNX conversion, benchmark testing before edge deployment

services.name: Azure Container Registry
services.purpose: Edge container images — ONNX runtime, quantized models, inference service, update agents

enterprise.note: 500GB, private endpoints, content trust, signed images

services.name: Blob Storage
services.purpose: Model registry — ONNX models, quantization artifacts, edge telemetry uploads, training data

services.name: Azure Event Grid
services.purpose: Model update notifications, device status events, deployment pipeline triggers

services.name: Azure Monitor
services.purpose: Edge device health, inference latency, model accuracy drift, deployment rollout tracking

services.name: Key Vault
services.purpose: Device provisioning keys, model signing certificates, IoT Hub connection strings

services.name: Azure Functions
services.purpose: Model conversion pipeline — trigger quantization jobs, validate ONNX exports, notify devices

enterprise.note: Always-warm, VNET integration, no cold start

optimization_tips: Use IoT Hub Free tier for dev with up to 500 devices — upgrade to S1 only when message volume exceeds 8K/day
Run model quantization in Container Instances on a schedule (nightly) instead of always-on — saves 70% vs dedicated compute
Use INT8 quantization for edge models — reduces model size 4x with <2% accuracy loss, saving bandwidth and storage costs
Implement delta model updates via IoT Hub — only push changed layers instead of full model files to reduce bandwidth costs
Tier telemetry storage with lifecycle policies — move inference logs to Cool storage after 7 days, Archive after 30 days
Use Event Grid filtering to limit notifications to only deployment-critical events — reduces unnecessary function invocations
